person.name: Thomas Eaton
person.userCertifiedLifeSummary:
«b»Marriage and Children«/b»
Married: Helen. Her maiden name is unknown.

«b»Children of Thomas and Helen Eaton:«/b»
1.) Daughter Eaton. The will of Thomas Eaton mentions son-in-law Richard Dallisbye and grandaughter Anna Dallisbye. This child's name and baptism was not found.

2.) John Eaton. bp. 26 December 1590 at Hatton. Immigrated to New England in 1639. Married 1st Anne Unknown; married 2nd Phebe Unknown. Died 29 October 1668 at Haverhill, Massachusetts.

3.) Thomas Eaton. bp. 25 December 1592 at Hatton. Presumably died before 1601, when a another son of Thomas and Helen Eaton was named Thomas.

4.) Richard Eaton. bp. 18 February 1594 at Hatton. Named in father's will on 24 June 1622.

5.) Job Eaton. bp. 29 August 1596 at Hatton. He married Alice by whom he had 5 known children.

6.) Elizabeth Eaton. bp. 11 February 1598/9 at Hatton. Named in father's will on 24 June 1622.

7.) Thomas Eaton bp. 30 August 1601 at Hatton. He married Margaret Woodward on 27 January 1628/9 at Hatton. Three known children.

8.) Sarah Eaton. bp. 5 November 1603 at Hatton. Named in father's will on 24 June 1622.

9.) Rachel Eaton. bp. 23 March 1604/5 at Hatton. Buried 21 October 1625 at Hatton

10.) Samuel Eaton bp. 1 June 1608 at Hatton. Named in father's will on 24 June 1622.

11.) Nathaniel Eaton. bp. 10 June 1610 at Hatton. He married 1st Anges by whom he had a son Robert bp. 15 September 1639; she was buried shortly after on 27 September 1639. He married 2nd Barbara Slater on 27 April 1640 at Whitechurch, Warwickshire.

«b»Death and Will«/b»
«b»Died:«/b» 5 November 1622 at Hatton, Warwickshire, England.
«b»Buried:«/b» 6 November 1622 at Hatton, Warwickshire, England.

«b»Will:«/b»
Thomas Eaton's will was executed June 24, 1622 and proved in 1623: the inventory was taken on Nov. 14, 1622. An abstract follows:

Thomas Eaton of Bewsall in parish of Hatton, Co. Warwick, yeoman, sick in body. To poor of Bewsall-10 s, To son Richard one piece of gold in value 11s, and 4 silver spoons. To son Job Eaton 3 pounds 6s. 8d. To daughter Elizabeth Eaton 1s. To son Thomas Eaton 5 pounds. To daughters Sara and Rachel and to son Samuel 20 pounds each to be paid by eldest son John Eaton. To son Nathaniel Eaton 1 sheep. To son John the table boards with the frame and benches standing in the hall and the same in the parlor, 1 great candlesticks, testator's great Bible, and malt mill. To daughters Sara and Rachell Eaton, one half of all household stuff unbequeathed. wife Helen to have use of it during her life. If children die before their parts are due, their shares to be divided among the surviving children. To wife Helen, one half of the moiety of all houses, barns, etc, if she remain unmarried. Residue of goods to wife Helen and she to be sole executrix. Overseers: son in law Richarde Dallisbye and son Richard Eaton: they to receive 5 s. each for their pains. To grandchild John Eaton, 1 sheep and to grandchild Anna Dallisbye, 1 sheep. Witnesses: Walter Terrill and Nicholas Rawboy.
Inventory taken Nov. 14, 1622 - 56 pounds, 7s, 10d.

zh.lifeSummary: 當Thomas Eaton於1565年出生在英國瓦維其可爾哈頓時，他的父親Thomas Eaton11562歲，母親Isabel Baker30歲。他於大約1587年與Mrs. Helen Eaton結婚。他們至少是7個兒子和4個女兒的父母。他去世於1622年11月5日之前在他的家鄉，埋葬在英國瓦維其可爾哈頓。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Thomas Eaton于1565年出生在英国瓦維其可爾哈頓时，他的父亲Thomas Eaton11562岁，母亲Isabel Baker30岁。他于大约1587年与Mrs. Helen Eaton结婚。他们至少是7个儿子和4个女儿的父母。他去世于1622年11月5日之前在他的家乡，埋葬在英国瓦維其可爾哈頓。

nameMeanings.name: Eaton
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from any of various places called Eaton or Eton, such as Eaton Socon (Bedfordshire), Eaton (Cheshire), or Eton (Buckinghamshire), named from either Old English ēa ‘river’ or ēg ‘island, low-lying land’ + tūn ‘enclosure, settlement’.
History: Nathaneal Eaton, born in Coventry, England, c. 1609, came to MA in 1637 and was the first head of Harvard College, in 1638–39.

nameMeanings.name: Thomas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: New Testament name, borne by one of Christ's twelve apostles, referred to as ‘Thomas, called Didymus’ (John 11:16; 20:24). Didymos is the Greek word for ‘twin’, and the name is the Greek form of an Aramaic byname meaning ‘twin’. The given name has always been popular throughout Christendom, in part because St Thomas's doubts have made him seem a very human character.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

en.lifeSummary: When Thomas Eaton was born in 1565, in Hatton, Warwickshire, England, his father, Thomas Eaton, was 11562 and his mother, Isabel Baker, was 30. He married Mrs. Helen Eaton about 1587. They were the parents of at least 7 sons and 4 daughters. He died before 5 November 1622, in his hometown, and was buried in Hatton, Warwickshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Thomas Eaton est né en 1565, à Hatton, Warwickshire, Angleterre, son père, Thomas Eaton, avait 11562 ans et sa mère, Isabel Baker, avait 30 ans. Il a épousé Mrs. Helen Eaton environ 1587. Ils ont eu au moins 7 fils et 4 filles. Il est décédé avant 5 novembre 1622, dans sa ville natale, et a été enterré à Hatton, Warwickshire, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Thomas Eaton 1565 in Hatton, Warwickshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Thomas Eaton 11562 und seine Mutter Isabel Baker 30. Er hat um 1587 Mrs. Helen Eaton geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 7 Söhne und 4 Töchter. Er starb vor 5. November 1622 in seinem Geburtsort und wurde in Hatton, Warwickshire, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Thomas Eaton è nato il nel 1565 nel Hatton, Warwickshire, England, il suo padre, Thomas Eaton, aveva 11562 anni e il suo madre, Isabel Baker, aveva 30 anni. Lui sposato Mrs. Helen Eaton circa 1587. Erano genitori di almeno 7 figli e 4 figlie. Morì prima del 5 novembre 1622, nel sua cittadina, e fu sepolto a Hatton, Warwickshire, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Thomas Eatonが1565年にイギリスワーウィクシュアイアハットオンで生まれた時，父Thomas Eatonは11562歳，母Isabel Bakerは30歳であった。約1587年にMrs. Helen Eatonと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも7人の息子と4人の娘の親であった。彼は1622年11月5日以前に故郷で死去し，イギリスワーウィクシュアイアハットオンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Thomas Eaton(이)가 1565년 영국 워윅실르 해턴에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Thomas Eaton(은)는 11562세였고, 그의 어머니 Isabel Baker(은)는 30세였다. 그는 대략 1587년 Mrs. Helen Eaton(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 일곱 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1622년 11월 5일 이전 그의 고향에서 사망했으며 영국 워윅실르 해턴에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Thomas Eaton nasceu em 1565, em Hatton, Warwickshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Thomas Eaton, tinha 11562 anos e sua mãe, Isabel Baker, tinha 30 anos. Ele casou-se com Mrs. Helen Eaton aproximadamente 1587. Eles tiveram pelo menos 7 filhos e 4 filhas. Ele faleceu antes 5 de novembro de 1622, em sua cidade natal, e foi sepultado em Hatton, Warwickshire, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Thomas Eaton родился в 1565 г. в местности хаттон, варвиккшир, Англия, его отцу по имени Thomas Eaton было 11562 года, а матери, которую звали Isabel Baker, 30. Он женился на женщине по имени Mrs. Helen Eaton около 1587 г. У них было по крайней мере 7 сыновей и 4 дочери. Он умер перед 5 ноябрь 1622 г. в его родном городе и был похоронен в местности хаттон, варвиккшир, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Thomas Eaton nació en 1565, en Hatton, Warwickshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Thomas Eaton, tenía 11562 años y su mamá, Isabel Baker, tenía 30 años. Se casó con Mrs. Helen Eaton aproximadamente 1587. Fueron padres de por lo menos 7 hijos y 4 hijas. Murió antes de 5 de noviembre de 1622, en su pueblo, y fue sepultado en Hatton, Warwickshire, Inglaterra.
